LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NORTHWEST CONVERTING CO. DBA PREMIER MOP AND BROOM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT NORTHWEST CONVERTING CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, IT MUST PREVAIL ON BOTH CLAIMS ALLEGED AGAINST IT IN ORDER FOR ITS MOTION TO BE GRANTED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY SOUNDING IN NEGLIGENCE TO THE PLAINTIFF. SEE DEFENDANT DEPOT'S RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FACTS 8, 12, 14 AND 16. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT NORTHWEST CONVERTING CO. dba PREMIER MOP AND BROOM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Nestor R. Schnasse, Esq. of Strick Law Offices 721-1200 for pltf & opposing party; James D. Biernat, Esq. of Law Office of James D. Biernat 650/802-0410 for deft & moving party; John C. Hentschel, Esq. of Livingston Law Firm 925/952-9880 for deft Restaurant Depot, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Northwest Converting Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Because defendant did not move for summary adjudication, it must prevail on both claims alleged against it in order for its motion to be granted. There are triable issues of fact whether defendant owed a duty sounding in negligence to the plaintiff. See Defendant Depot's responses to plaintiff's facts 8, 12, 14 and 16. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
